rulings.Case Title: WALSH VS PESKIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Under Ccp Section 425.16 (B) And For Costs & Attorney'S Fees; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: The court finds that allegations concerning actions in defense of original CC&R lawsuit are subject to special motions to strike and modifies its ruling on previous special motion to strike to extent necessary to reflect this finding. The hearing on this portion of the special motion to strike is continued 90 days to allow plaintiff discovery regarding its prima facie case. Supplemental opposition due fifteen days prior to the hearing and supplemental reply due six days prior to hearing. The special motion to strike regarding allegation that Peskin contacted tenants to inform them about their rights as tenants is STAYED pending decision of court of appeal. Demurrer to 1st cause of action Breach of Fiduciary Duty is OVERRULED because Defendant has failed to sustain its burden of showing that the entire claim is barred by the litigation privilege. Demurrer to Second cause of action for breach of contract is OVERRULED because Defendant has failed to sustain its burden of showing that the entire claim is barred by the litigation privilege. Demurrer to 3rd cause of action Conspiracy; 4th cause of action Negligence; 5th cause of action Intentional Tort; 7th cause of action Mismanagement of Non-Profit Corp; and 8th and 9th causes of action for injunction are STAYED pending resolution of the special motion to strike. Demurrer to 6th cause of action Retaliatory Eviction SUSTAINED without leave. Defendants are not Plaintiff's landlords. (CW)
